courseDetails.longDescription:
This course contains the use of artificial intelligence.

Imagine you’re a junior HR professional aiming to open doors to international companies. You’re reading articles, watching webinars, going through books — but everything feels fragmented. There’s no clear logic, no structure, no understanding of which skills truly matter or how to prove your qualifications.


And then, for the first time, you hear about CIPD — the most recognized HR qualification in the world. But along with curiosity comes fear: complex assignments, strict academic style, evidence requirements, tens of pages of instructions. Many students feel lost before they even begin preparing.


This course was created to guide you through CIPD Level 3 confidently, clearly, and without chaos. Over eight sessions, you’ll cover all the content required for People Practice and get a simple, step-by-step preparation plan for your assignments — from choosing a topic to final formatting.


It’s a course that replaces months of trying to guess “what exactly do they want from me?” and turns CIPD into an achievable goal even for a beginner.


Here’s what the programme includes:


Section 1. Introduction to CIPD and People Practice


• CIPD levels and structure

• How modules and assignments work

• Evidence-based practice and academic requirements

• Fundamentals of People Practice: roles, skills, competencies


Section 2. Organisation, culture and change (3CO01)


• Strategies, structures, stakeholders

• PESTLE analysis

• Types of organisational culture

• Lewin’s and Kotter’s change models

• The role of HR in transformations


Section 3. HR Analytics and Metrics (3CO02)


• Quantitative and qualitative data

• Key HR metrics: turnover, engagement, absenteeism

• Data analysis and visualisation

• Using analytics in CIPD assignments


Section 4. HR Behaviour and Professional Standards (3CO03)


• CIPD Core Behaviours

• Ethics and professionalism

• Emotional intelligence and influence

• Reflection and HR professional development


Section 5. HR Practices: recruitment, onboarding, development, motivation (3CO04)


• Hiring: job descriptions, assessment, interviews

• Onboarding and adaptation

• Employee learning and development

• Performance management and development models

• Basics of Total Rewards, employee relations, conflict management


Section 6. Preparing for CIPD Level 3 Assignments


• Format, structure, and logic of assignments

• Academic writing: style, argumentation, references

• How to collect evidence

• Working with Turnitin

• Common CIPD student mistakes and how to avoid them


Why this course stands out:


• It doesn’t just explain theory — it shows how to complete CIPD assignments correctly

• You get real examples of Home Assignments

• You learn to write academically, as required by CIPD

• The structure mirrors the actual Level 3 modules

• You save months of trial and error


If you want to build a career in HR, enter the international market, and earn a recognized certificate, CIPD Level 3 is your starting point. And this course will help you take that path with confidence, clarity, and without costly mistakes.


Start your preparation today — the global HR community has a place for those who take the first step.
targetAudiences: Beginning HR professionals who want to obtain their first international qualification
Students and graduates planning to build a career in HR or People Practice

HR practitioners who lack consistency, analytics, and understanding of the CIPD model
Those who want to work in international companies and need a recognized certificate
Anyone who wants to complete CIPD Level 3 faster, more clearly, and with a high chance of passing.

courseDetails.title: COO Masterclass: Operatives Geschäft & Betriebsführung [DE]

Dieser umfassende Kurs wurde sorgfältig entwickelt, um angehenden und aktuellen COOs, Operations Directors und Führungskräften die notwendigen Fähigkeiten und Instrumente zu vermitteln, um im operativen Leadership zu brillieren. Durch eine Kombination aus theoretischem Wissen und praxisnahen Anwendungen lernen die Teilnehmenden, Geschäftsprozesse zu verbessern, eine leistungsorientierte Kultur zu fördern und Strategien umzusetzen, die nachhaltiges Wachstum ermöglichen.

• Organisationskultur gestalten: Aufbau einer Kultur, die Strategie unterstützt und Engagement fördert.

• HR-Profis, die ihre Rolle um operative Verantwortung erweitern möchten.

• CEOs und Unternehmer, die Effizienz und Profitabilität steigern wollen.

Das Programm umfasst 10 ausführliche Lektionen, jede zu einem zentralen Aspekt der COO-Rolle. Die Lektionen werden wöchentlich veröffentlicht, damit du das Wissen schrittweise anwenden kannst. Jede Einheit beinhaltet Video-Lektionen, praktische Aufgaben und persönliches Feedback.


Wichtige Module

  7. Exzellentes Projektmanagement

  8. Finanzmanagement für COOs

  1. Internet Status Checker: This project checks the online status of the user's device using JavaScript. The result is displayed on an HTML page styled with CSS. The project uses the navigator. Online property to check the online status of the user's device. If the device is online, the project displays the text "Online" on the page. If the device is offline, the project displays the text "Offline" on the page.

  2. Palindrome Checker: This project checks if a given string is a palindrome using JavaScript. The user inputs the string through an HTML form and the result is displayed on the page, styled with CSS. The project uses a recursive function to check if the string is a palindrome. The function starts at the beginning and end of the string and compares the characters at each index. If the characters are the same, the function continues to the next index. If the characters are not the same, the function returns false.

  3. RGB Color Generator: This project generates random RGB (red, green, blue) color values using JavaScript. The generated color is displayed on an HTML page, with the background color changing to the generated color. The page can be styled with CSS. The project uses the Math. random() function to generate random numbers between 0 and 255. The three random numbers are used to create an RGB color value. The color value is then used to change the background color of the page.

  4. Counter Application: This project is a simple counter application that uses JavaScript to increment and decrement a count value. The count value is displayed on an HTML page and buttons can be added to increment and decrement the count. The page can be styled with CSS. The project uses two variables to store the count value and the button click event handlers to increment and decrement the count value. The count value is then displayed on the page.

  5. ASCII and Unicode Character Detector: This project uses JavaScript to detect whether a given character is an ASCII or Unicode character. The user inputs the character through an HTML form and the result is displayed on the page, styled with CSS. The project uses the String.fromCharCode() function to convert the character to a string. The charCodeAt() function is then used to get the ASCII code of the character. If the ASCII code is between 0 and 127, the character is an ASCII character. Otherwise, the character is a Unicode character.

  6. Analog Clock Application: This project displays an analog clock with the current time on an HTML page using JavaScript. The clock is created using CSS to style the clock's face and hands. The project uses the Date() object to get the current time. The current time is then used to set the position of the clock's hands.

  7. Random Color Generator: This project generates random colors in any format (e.g. RGB, HEX, HSL) using JavaScript. The generated color is displayed on an HTML page, with the background color changing to the generated color. The page can be styled with CSS. The project uses the Math. random() function to generate random numbers between 0 and 255. The three random numbers are used to create an RGB color value. The color value is then converted to the desired format and displayed on the page.

  8. BMI Calculator Application: This project calculates the user's body mass index (BMI) based on their height and weight using JavaScript. The user inputs their height and weight through an HTML form and the result is displayed on the page, styled with CSS. The project uses the Math. round() function to round the BMI value to two decimal places. The BMI value is then displayed on the page.

  9. Loan Calculator: This project calculates the monthly payment and total interest for a loan based on the loan amount, interest rate, and loan term using JavaScript. The user inputs these values through an HTML form and the result is displayed on the page, styled with CSS. The project uses the Math. pow() function to calculate the monthly payment. The monthly payment is then multiplied by the loan term to calculate the total interest. The total interest is then displayed on the page.

  10. Love Calculator: This project calculates a "love percentage" between two people based on their names using JavaScript. The user inputs their names through an HTML form and the result is displayed on the page, styled with CSS. The project uses a simple algorithm to calculate the love percentage. The algorithm adds up the number of letters in each name and then divides the sum by 2. The love percentage is then displayed on the page.

courseDetails.title: 3D Printing with " Ultimaker CURA Slicer- Start to finish"
courseDetails.shortDescription: CURA, Slicing Software, 3D Printing , Ultimaker CURA , Additive Manufacturing, G-code, Digital Manufacturing
courseDetails.longDescription:
Cura 3D is the friendly face of slicing software, so much so that many users don’t even realize what it’s doing. Just load the model, select the quality, and hit print. It’s as easy as traditional 2D printing.

Essentially that’s all Cura is — print software — a way to get a digital file from your computer to the 3D printer in a format that the 3D printing hardware understands.

The program is not only free for you to download, it is also open source. It is the gold standard of 3D slicers worldwide. If you compare Cura to other 3D slicing software, it all seems very simple, with limited options and adjustments. But Cura’s more complex settings are there if you need them; it’s just been designed very neatly and user-friendly.

Cura is maintained by 3D printer manufacturer Ultimaker and – as we know from their hardware – they’re perfectionists. Hidden (but not too deeply) in the software are almost all those settings and options that you can find in the majority of other slicing software. So if you are one of those people who like to tinker with the 3D printer settings, you can.

For those that just want to print, change a few quality and speed options, then in Cura it’s all carefully laid out and ready to go.

This Cura software tutorial is aimed at anyone just starting out with 3D printing. We’re only going to assume that you’ve switched on the printer and loaded the filament. From that point on we’re here to help guide you in the ways of Cura 3D and getting started with 3D printing.
